your_book:
Tuesday's session handed Jaleel's book a modest +0.97% unrealized gain on a $21,820.92 account, but the headline masks a tale of two stories. The real driver was software: NOW surged +2.59% and ADBE +1.59% after Guggenheim pushed back on AI-disruption fears, arguing the market has been too pessimistic — a direct reprieve for ADBE's persistent narrative overhang. UBER (+2.64%) and GILD (+5.21%, the session's best single-day print) added further lift. On the defensive side, ABT barely moved at +0.22% and DHR +0.29% — steady, but not contributing much today. NVDA has no day-return data available for this session, leaving $1,426.91 worth of the book in a blind spot; it did flag in the whale-activity headlines, suggesting institutional activity worth tracking.

The divergence that demands attention is RKLB, which cratered -10.4% today and now sits -15.4% in the hole on cost basis — a $1,584.79 position rated AVOID with a QARP score of just 39.2. Price and thesis are not diverging here; they are aligned in the wrong direction. The whale-activity headline is noise without a clear catalyst, but a -10.4% single-session drop is not noise. Separately, LULU slipped another -0.49% and is now -10.0% on cost, carrying an elevated guidance-cut flag with Americas comps down five straight quarters. BSX is up today (+1.57%) but the guidance-slash flag — FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5% with flat guidance through Q3 — means today's green is borrowing against a weak fundamental setup ahead of the July 29 print.

Watch the software cluster closely: NOW's July 22 earnings are the next real test of whether the Guggenheim thesis holds or was just a one-day bounce. ADBE's -9.7% unrealized loss still needs the numbers to reassert themselves against the narrative. The book is capital-preservation-minded but is carrying two loss positions (ADBE, RKLB) above 7% of portfolio weight combined — that concentration in underwater names deserves an honest review.

attention.headline: –10.4% single-session drop on an already –15.4% position rated AVOID
attention.note: A one-day -10.4% move in a position the model already rates AVOID with a QARP of 39.2 demands an explicit hold-or-exit decision; continuing to carry it is itself a choice.

attention.headline: No session return data available despite whale activity flagged
attention.note: NVDA is a $1,426.91 position with an active export-controls risk flag and institutional whale activity noted today — the absence of a day return leaves the session's impact unaccounted for.

attention.headline: Up today but earnings on July 29 follow a guidance slash to 7–8.5% growth with flat Q3 outlook
attention.note: Today's +1.57% is a short-term positive against a deteriorating fundamental backdrop; the July 29 print is a binary event that could re-test the –5.4% cost-basis loss.

attention.headline: Five straight quarters of negative Americas comps with tariff-driven margin pressure
attention.note: Another down day (-0.49%) compounds a -10.0% unrealized loss on a position whose next catalyst (Sep 2) is still two months out with no near-term repair in sight.
